mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate sets the floor for short-term rates; a higher FEDFUNDS level lifts the entire yield curve, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise over the next 20 trading days. This relationship holds continuously regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: The 10-year yield falls within 20 trading days after FEDFUNDS rises, in more than 30% of occurrences over any 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate exceeding 5% signals economic weakening and flight to safety, driving investors toward Treasuries and pushing yields lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Yields rise within 20 trading days after UNRATE exceeds 5.0% in more than 20% of triggered instances over a 3-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in both retail sales and housing starts for two consecutive months indicate broad consumer demand weakness, reducing economic growth expectations and causing the 10-year yield to fall over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines MoM and HOUST declines MoM for two consecutive months
failure_condition: The 10-year yield rises within 20 trading days after the joint decline condition is met, in more than 25% of occurrences over any 2-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarter-over-quarter increases in industrial production and real GDP reflect strong economic expansion, raising inflation expectations and pushing the 10-year yield higher over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases QoQ and GDPC1 increases QoQ
failure_condition: The 10-year yield falls within 60 trading days after both series increase quarter-over-quarter, in more than 30% of cases over any 3-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 3% year-over-year, investors demand higher term premiums to compensate for eroding purchasing power, causing the 10-year yield to rise over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 10-year yield falls within 20 trading days after CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3%, in more than 20% of triggered instances over a 2-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle defined by the federal funds rate above 2.5% and rising by at least 25bp in the past month, an increase in housing starts signals demand resilience that amplifies inflationary pressure, leading to higher 10-year yields over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS up at least 25bp in last month
failure_condition: The 10-year yield falls within 20 trading days after HOUST rises while FEDFUNDS > 2.5% and rising, in more than 25% of occurrences over any 2-year walk-forward during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A higher level of industrial production indicates robust economic activity, which increases inflation expectations and pushes the 10-year yield higher over the next 20 trading days, regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: The 10-year yield falls within 20 trading days when INDPRO is above its 1-year median, in more than 40% of cases over any 3-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth accelerates above a 3% annualized rate quarter-over-quarter, the economy is overheating, leading to expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher yields over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The 10-year yield declines within 60 trading days after GDP growth exceeds 0.75% QoQ, in more than 30% of occurrences over any 3-year walk-forward.
